courseTitle: 10th Annual Southwest Regional Pulmonary/PCCM Fellow EBUS Course
hostLine: Hosted by Loma Linda University Health
hostDepartment: Division of Pulmonary, Critical Care, Hyperbaric and Sleep Medicine
venueDetail: Loma Linda University School of Medicine
audience: Designed to set expectations early for first-year pulmonary and pulmonary-critical care fellows who are building the cognitive and technical foundations of EBUS.
overview: A flipped, simulation-first regional EBUS course that helps fellows arrive with the staging logic, anatomy framework, and procedural muscle memory needed for a more standardized mediastinal evaluation.

quickFacts.label: annual regional course
quickFacts.detail: A longstanding Southwest fellowship training experience.

quickFacts.label: pre-course video prep
quickFacts.detail: High-yield lectures are distributed 3 to 4 weeks before the live day.

quickFacts.label: fellow-to-faculty ratio
quickFacts.detail: Small groups keep feedback direct and hands-on.

quickFacts.label: regional faculty
quickFacts.detail: Faculty span LLU, Stanford, Cedars-Sinai, USC, UCSD, UCLA, UCI, and more.

positioningHighlights: Built specifically for first-year pulmonary and PCCM fellows.
The only Southwest course of its kind pairing comprehensive didactics with live EBUS simulation.
Focused on standardizing both cognitive staging strategy and technical execution early in fellowship.
facultySummary: Faculty listed on the flyer span LLU, Stanford, Cedars-Sinai, USC, UCSD, City of Hope, UCLA, UCI, Harbor-UCLA, Scripps, Eisenhower, UNLV, UT Southwestern, Navy, and the University at Buffalo.
formatHighlights: Flipped model with more than 15 hours of high-yield pre-course lectures prepared by faculty.
Live day is almost entirely simulation-based instead of lecture-heavy.
Small-group teaching model with a low fellow-to-faculty ratio.
Dedicated time for technology exposure, networking, and direct faculty interaction.
App-based pre- and post-testing with feedback is new for the 2026 course.

experienceHighlights.title: Flipped preparation
experienceHighlights.detail: Faculty-created video lectures cover the core knowledge base before fellows step into the simulation center.

experienceHighlights.title: Simulation-first live day
experienceHighlights.detail: The in-person session is built around stations, coached repetition, and deliberate practice rather than a lecture-heavy agenda.

experienceHighlights.title: Gamified anatomy and review
experienceHighlights.detail: The flyer highlights a mediastinal anatomy game up front and a Final Jeopardy-style close to reinforce retention.

experienceHighlights.title: Low learner-to-faculty ratio
experienceHighlights.detail: Small rotating teams create room for real-time correction, questions, and technique coaching.

experienceHighlights.title: Technology and networking time
experienceHighlights.detail: Protected breaks are built into the day for hands-on technology exposure and fellow-faculty interaction.

experienceHighlights.title: App-based testing
experienceHighlights.detail: The 2026 course introduces pre- and post-testing with feedback to tighten the learning loop.

prepWindow: Preparatory materials are distributed 3 to 4 weeks before the live event and should be completed between May 1 and May 30, 2026.
prepTopics: Welcome, introduction to the course, and fellow recognition.
Introduction to endobronchial ultrasound and the procedure nuts and bolts.
Mediastinal anatomy plus systematic lung cancer staging using TNM 9.
Approach to lymphoma and non-malignant thoracic disease.
Histology, molecular testing, and tissue adequacy.
Liquid biopsy, advanced EBUS techniques, and the 2026 evidence update.
Guideline-based planning, case synthesis, and the pre-course test.

liveSessionTracks.title: AM tools and techniques
liveSessionTracks.detail: Teams rotate through the mediastinal anatomy game, scope anatomy and assembly, the EBUS exam, standard needles and TBNA technique, plus advanced tools.

liveSessionTracks.title: Technology and networking break
liveSessionTracks.detail: The morning schedule protects dedicated time in the main conference area for industry exposure and conversations across programs.

liveSessionTracks.title: Midday honorary lecture
liveSessionTracks.detail: Lunch includes the AABIP guidelines session with Dr. Russell Miller before the afternoon synthesis block.

liveSessionTracks.title: PM synthesis and execution
liveSessionTracks.detail: The afternoon shifts into case-based planning, advanced cases and techniques, and technical execution before the Final Jeopardy close.

liveDayAgenda.title: Registration and breakfast
liveDayAgenda.detail: First floor of the Centennial Complex before team rotations begin.

liveDayAgenda.title: Welcome and course overview
liveDayAgenda.detail: All fellows meet in Damazo Auditorium.

liveDayAgenda.title: AM tools and techniques rotations
liveDayAgenda.detail: Mediastinal anatomy game, scope anatomy and assembly, EBUS exam, standard needles and TBNA technique, plus advanced tools.

liveDayAgenda.title: Technology and networking break
liveDayAgenda.detail: Protected time in the main conference area.

liveDayAgenda.title: Lunch and honorary lecture
liveDayAgenda.detail: AABIP guidelines session with Dr. Russell Miller.

liveDayAgenda.title: PM synthesis and execution rotations
liveDayAgenda.detail: Case-based planning, advanced cases and techniques, then technical execution.

liveDayAgenda.title: Final Jeopardy and farewell
liveDayAgenda.detail: All fellows reconvene in Damazo Auditorium to close the day.

parkingNote: Parking is directed to lot X off Campus Street.
travelNote: The flyer includes separate driving directions for attendees coming from Los Angeles, Palm Springs, San Diego, and Las Vegas.

travelDirections.detail: Take I-10 East to the Tippecanoe/Anderson exit, continue to Stewart Street, then turn onto Campus Street and park in lot X.
Take I-10 West to Tippecanoe/Anderson, head to Stewart Street, then Campus Street, and use lot X.
Take I-15 North to I-215 North, exit Mt Vernon, continue on Washington/Barton to Campus Street, and park in lot X.
Take I-15 South to I-10 East, exit at Tippecanoe/Anderson, then follow Stewart Street to Campus Street and lot X.

logo.alt: Loma Linda University School of Medicine Department of Medicine logo

hero.alt: Faculty coaching fellows during a hands-on bronchoscopy and ultrasound simulation station.
hero.caption: Hands-on coaching is a central part of the live day.

inset.alt: Regional fellows and faculty gathered outside the course venue.
inset.caption: Regional fellows and faculty come together across programs.

gallery.alt: Multiple fellow groups rotating through a large simulation bay filled with bronchoscopy equipment.
gallery.caption: Small rotating teams keep the live day active, social, and skills-focused.

gallery.alt: Fellows and faculty gathered during an end-of-day recognition moment inside the classroom space.
gallery.caption: The course is designed to feel coached and collegial from first welcome to final debrief.

gallery.alt: A large outdoor group portrait of fellows and faculty from prior EBUS courses.
gallery.caption: Regional faculty help fellows compare technique, workflow, and staging habits across programs.
